product_summary: The Schedule Product Deliveries (MTO) agent automates the alignment of inbound material deliveries with make-to-order production schedules by coordinating supplier windows and production start dates. It leverages hybrid capabilities to validate schedules, generate expedite alerts, track supplier confirmations, and handle exceptions in real-time. This ensures seamless supply chain operations, reduces production downtime, and maintains compliance with industry standards such as GxP for pharma and GDPR for personal data. Executives benefit from improved on-time delivery rates and optimized inventory management, driving cost efficiencies and customer satisfaction.
value_proposition: Automates coordination of supplier delivery windows with production schedules to eliminate misalignment delays and ensure reliable make-to-order fulfillment.
technical_summary: The hybrid agent integrates with ERP, MES, and supplier portal systems via APIs to execute schedule validation, adjustment, and confirmation tracking through combined rule-based engines and predictive models. It uses event-driven workflows for expedite alerts and exception handling while embedding compliance modules that enforce GxP, contractual, and GDPR requirements during data exchanges. Architecture supports both automated orchestration and human-in-the-loop escalation for complex cases.
integration_guide:
# Integration Guide — mto_delivery_scheduler
**Process:** Schedule Product Deliveries (MTO)
**Version:** 1.0.0

## Prerequisites
- Python 3.10+
- Agentic Zero runtime installed
- API credentials configured in .env

## Installation
```bash
# Copy agent to your project
cp mto_delivery_scheduler.py ./agents/
```

## Basic Usage
```python
from agents.mto_delivery_scheduler import MtoDeliverySchedulerAgent

agent = MtoDeliverySchedulerAgent()
result = agent.execute({
    "production_orders": your_production_orders_data,
    "supplier_lead_times": your_supplier_lead_times_data,
    "material_requirements": your_material_requirements_data,
})
print(result['outputs'])
```

## Supported Systems
- SAP ECC
- SAP S/4HANA
- SAP EWM
- Oracle ERP Cloud
- Oracle JDE

## Tools Required
- ERP_production_order_API
- supplier_portal_capacity_API
- MES_production_start_API
- logistics_transport_schedule_API

## Escalation
The agent automatically escalates to human when:
- Missing production_order data flagged for planner
- Lead time variance >10% or ExpediteAlert volume exceeds threshold
- Transportation delay >4h after automated reschedule attempt
api_endpoints: POST /api/v1/agents/mto_delivery_scheduler/execute — Execute the agent with inputs
GET  /api/v1/agents/mto_delivery_scheduler/status — Get agent status and metrics

use_cases: Use case 1: Automotive Tier-1 supplier uses MTO agent to auto-sequence custom EV battery pack deliveries, cutting late-shipment penalties by 32% and saving $1.2M annually in contractual fines.

Use case 2: Defense contractor schedules made-to-order avionics modules with the agent enforcing ITAR and contractual milestone dates, reducing schedule slippage from 18% to 4% and avoiding $850K in liquidated damages per program.

Use case 3: Chemical manufacturer applies the agent to coordinate MTO specialty resin tank deliveries under GxP documentation rules, lowering demurrage and rush freight costs by 21% ($640K/year) while maintaining full audit trails.

Use case 4: Industrial equipment OEM uses the agent for complex MTO turbine generator shipments, improving on-time delivery from 76% to 94% and increasing repeat-order revenue by $2.3M through higher customer retention.

Use case 5: Precision manufacturing firm integrates the agent to align MTO custom tooling deliveries with GDPR-compliant customer data handling, reducing expedited shipping spend by 27% ($410K/year) via predictive slotting.
